reviews.title: really nice keycaps
reviews.text: i bought these because i always wanted pudding style keycaps for my rgb keyboard, they look really great and the light shines through really well. the quality and material is also good for the price

title: KINESIS USB-A Freestyle Pro Quiet Ergonomic Split Mechanical Keyboard (Cherry MX Silent Red Switches)
features: AWARD-WINNING SPLIT DESIGN: Advanced ergonomics for all body types and preferences via adjustable split, splay, and tent (tenting accessories sold separately). New cable storage compartment supports up to 20" of separation when you need it.

GENUINE CHERRY MECHANICAL SWITCHES FOR PREMIUM TYPING FEEL AND PERFORMANCE WITH LESS NOISE: Cherry MX "Silent" red mechanical switches feature a low activation force, linear feel and are rated for 50M key presses for unmatched durability.

FULL ONBOARD PROGRAMMABILITY WITH THE DRIVERLESS SMARTSET ENGINE: 9 custom layouts, 8 office hotkeys, on-the-fly macro recording, one-touch key remapping, pre-configured Mac and Dvorak layouts, settings saved to keyboards 4MB flash memory, and the SmartSet App GUI for Windows & Mac.
PLUG-AND-PLAY WITH ALL MAJOR OPERATING SYSTEMS: Windows 7-10, Mac OS X, Linux, Chrome.
2 YEAR MANUFACTURER'S WARRANTY: Buy with confidence knowing that Kinesis engineers all our products in the USA to the highest standards and stands behind them 100 percent since 1992.

reviews.title: Good, but could be better
reviews.text:
I use this with my MacBook Pro. I have brutal pain in my hands, wrists and forearms from osteoarthritis, tendon-patchy and tendonitis; caused from 19 years of being at my computer and holding my phone up for blogging.

This keyboard has made things so much better, however... I needed the keyboard to have the lift without the wrist pads which are agony for my hands. Only, the tent system is made to include all pieces; the lift and the wrist pads. So, I have to use the lift pieces without the wrist pads which leaves a chunk of awkward plastic sticking straight out from the keyboard; something I am constantly catching. It's frustrating to no end.

I am now looking for something I can simply glue to the inner, underside of the keyboard that will add the lift but leave the front open.

I'm also disappointed with the price. Come on now. It's sooooo overpriced. It's only a matter of time before another brand comes out with a more reasonable one- but in the meantime, I feel like they are taking advantage.

In the end, this is far better than nothing. It definitely places your hands in a more ergonomic position, and is better than a flat keyboard or laptop touchpad setup.

reviews.title: Great keyboard for ergonomics
reviews.text: The split keyboard layout has been great for my shoulders and arms.  It took a couple of weeks for me to get used to the layout but the extra functions offered on the edges of the keyboard are very helpful.  Being able to replace the key caps is huge.  I really love this keyboard.

reviews.title: Quality material, but as wide as TKL keyboards
reviews.text:
I wish the length of the cable was longer, and that a longer cable can be purchased separately.
For the price, the tenting should be standard, but it is a separate purchase. And, without them, this keyboard isn't as great.

I purchased the tenting separately, and it really helps with ergonomics when typing. But, when gaming it tends to push my hands side and down, so it's better to use a separate palm support.

I was surprised that when the keyboard is laid out, it is as wide as a ten keyless keyboard. I was hoping that it would be shorter, so that the mouse has more room. It's fine for work or just typing. For gaming, I'll put the right section farther up. Or, I'll put it above the keyboard tray, so I still have access to the keys when needed. That frees up a lot of room for the mouse.

The quality of the keyboard is great. Everything feels solid. The keys feel wonderful. And, the ergonomics is great for typing.
The tech support was friendly and helpful. I had a bit of a problem early on with customizing the keys, and their tech support promptly helped me.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: Nice quiet keyboard with a great feel.  I got the tenting and wrist rest kit.  It took a little bit to get used to the angle but I'm really enjoying it and have already programmed some of the keys to do routine things for me.

reviews.title: Perfect balance of ergonomics and key comfort.
reviews.text:
I have tried all the Kinesis keyboards with the exception of the 400$ console setup. I have chosen this one as the best overall experience, specifically paired with the VIP3Pro tent and wrist accessory. The keys on this keyboard have a much more consistent and pleasant feeling as compared to their membrane offerings as well as their silent switch option. Those keyboards are fantastic if you are not already addicted to the feeling of mechanical keys. However if you are searching for the ultimate in keyboard experiences for daily use, I heavily and without reservation recommend this keyboard set. The keyboard may initially seem expensive when compared to other ergonomic keyboards that do not split or offer the tenting/wrist rests, however having tried many different curved and non split ergonomic options from Microsoft, Logitech, and many others, I can say without a doubt that the optimal configuration requires the keyboard to be split. If you need to have a number pad like I do, I suggest any simple number key option, they'll fit nicely between the two halves and do not interfere at all. I plan to purchase a second keyboard for my WFH setup to compliment my work setup. I don't want to go back to a standard keyboard and you won't either after using it.

This review was not endorsed, sponsored, or influenced by any 3rd party, I am simply that overjoyed with the product.

title: Redragon K556 RGB Mechanical Gaming Keyboard M612 Wired Gaming Mouse Bundle
features: This bundle includes a K556 gaming keyboard and a M612 Mouse.

K556 Keyboard: The mechanical brown switch offers the best tactile typing experience, also being ever so slightly quieter, fitting for office or gaming use. And you will get 8 spared switches as a gift.

K556 Keyboard: RGB LED adjustable lighting with 6-themed backlights -18 lighting models. You can also adjust their brightness with FN+“↑”and“↓” Besides, there is a 9-color single backlit mode for this keyboard.

M612 Mouse: Pentakill, 5 DPI Levels - Geared with 5 redefinable DPI levels (default as: 500/1000/2000/3000/4000), easy to switch between different game needs. Dedicated demand of DPI options between 500-8000 is also available to be processed by software.

M612 Mouse: Any Button is Reassignable - 11 programmable buttons are all editable with customizable tactical keybinds in whatever game or work you are engaging. 1 rapid fire + 2 side macro buttons offer you a better gaming and working experience.

reviews.title: Small kickstands
reviews.text: The kickstands or keyboard legs are short so even when they are up, keyboard still feels pretty small. Other than that, really good keyboard.

reviews.title: Good quality, thick keycaps
reviews.text: Fits my case perfectly. Feels nice and looks nice.

reviews.title: Good quality and adorable!
reviews.text:
Key caps are textured, but do not feel rough. The textured PBT will keep them from getting shiny as fast (compared to smooth ABS). No print errors. Fit the Cherry switches perfectly. They feel weighty and durable. The plastic is cut cleanly, no rough edges. Nice big font, centered on each key. I am very happy with these.

The only thing I am a bit disappointed about is that the special (non-standard) Capslock key does not fit my keyboard; the slot where you are supposed to insert the switch is not centered correctly. But, it isn't enough of a disappointment for me to knock down my rating.

reviews.title: Perfect!
reviews.text: These keycaps are made of PBT, which is about the highest grade of plastic that you can get for your keycaps. I love the flatter profile of the XDA keycaps. The print on the keys is very classic, large, and easy to read. This keycap set contains both peach and sea green keycaps for the function keys, space bar, escape, and enter keys. You can mix and match them until your hearts delight. You even get some extra keys for media keys if your keyboard has them, plus menu and function keys. You get a wide variety of keys that will fit most Cherry MX style keyboards easily. The plastic is nice and textured so your fingers won't slip off the keycaps when you are typing on the keyboard. I would highly recommend these keycaps as an upgrade to ABS keycaps, and for customizing your keyboard in a cute vintage style.

title: Code V3 104-Key Illuminated Mechanical Keyboard - White LED Backlighting, Black Case (Cherry MX Brown)
features: White LED backlighting w/7 levels of brightness. Onboard memory saves your lighting preference
Fully programmable via hardware: program macros and keymaps over four layers
No obnoxious branding with programmable RGB indicator lights
Slim case design w/multi-channel cable routing system and detachable USB cable

reviews.title: Buy it
reviews.text:
If you use a keyboard 8 hours a day, make it count. Use a keyboard that you enjoy.
The Code keyboard is one of my favorites. The clear switches make it so you have a great feel - but don’t annoy your coworkers.

title: Gaming Keyboard and Mouse Combo, MageGee GK770 RGB Backlit Wired Gaming Keyboard with Multimedia Keys, Ergonomic Wrist Rest Computer Keyboard, 3200 DPI Gaming Mouse for Windows PC Gamers

Accurate, comfortable, soft touch with anti-ghosting functionality. RGB backlit effect provides a unique visual experience, taking you into a pretty cool gaming atmosphere. ✅10 MULTIMEDIA KEYS & 25 NON-CONFLICT KEYS: 10 independent multimedia keys at the top of the keyboard makes it quick access to music player, email, computer, calculator without interrupting your game. 25 anti-ghosting keys allow you to press or hold multiple keys simultaneously, helping you win in the fierce game without being bound by speed. Adjustable rear feet and non-slip pads provide comfortable typing experience.

✅RGB LED BACKLIGHT KEYBOARD: The keyboard has RGB light settings with multiple modes (dynamic backlit or solid static light) to choose from, making it shining and sparkling in the dark, easy to find the correct keys and reduce error rate. Advanced gaming optical sensor ensures accurate positioning and stable transmission. Pressing the DPI button for 3 seconds can turn off the mouse backlight. ✅WIDE COMPATIBILITY: The USB wired keyboard and mouse combo is simple to use, just plug and play, no need for additional driver or software. Works well with Win2000, Win XP, WinME, Vista, Win7, Win8, Android, linux, Mac and other operating systems. Suitable for gaming beginners or lovers.

reviews.title: Where's the documentation?
reviews.text: Very heavy keyboard. Certainly doesn't feel like a mechanical keyboard. The tiny insert offers ZERO CLUES as to how the keyboard is supposed to function, ie there is no documentation. The web site reads more like an ad, no clues there, either. The flashing color LEDs remind me of a 1980s video arcade game. You can reduce the brightness down to zero but that's about it. There's a really ugly and difficult to read quasi-sci-fi typeface used on the key caps. The keyboard itself is quite nice, quite heavy. Little kickstand-type feet if you want it angled. A small plastic ramp is included if  you want to rest  your left hand. What's missing here is a MANUAL of some kind. Even a PDF would be just great. Otherwise it's a mystery how this keyboard is supposed to work, what the features are. Somebody blew it. It's nicely designed with some interesting features, but unless you enjoy hardware sleuthing, how are you supposed to know what works, and how it works? Rather maddening. So I don't see this being used, I'll go back to my older keyboard which actually has mechanical clickity click key responses. It's baffling that with  the attention to detail paid to this keyboard that they wouldn't include or provide some DOCUMENTATION. It's basic.

reviews.title: This is an amazing keyboard! With this and the mouse, I am ecstatic! Don't hesitate on this! Can I give it 6 stars?
reviews.text:
Oh wow! I have waited for several days to review this because my computer was broken and I had to wait for a service tech to drive down and DO something, ANYTHING, with it. When the box came, I was asking myself what it was because the box was HUGE, The inner box was still pretty big, but not huge, It provides a lot of information--including that the keyboard is waterproof, nice since I'm a klutz--and something I'd either not noticed or had forgotten. In side that was a plain black box with a Bloody hand--or is it a hand print? The lights are cool, and the bloody hand lights up on both the keyboard and the Bloody Mouse I got a couple of days before the keyboard arrived. The fast key response is amazing. Even though I'm not a "gamer," I'm not a total computer novice, having gotten my first one with a gig on the hard drive--custom built--and my co-workers said I'd never use all that memory. Right. Now, my phone has 64 times that with room for expansion and does more than that first computer--or the second--and maybe more than the fifth or sixth. I still have to download the software, which will probably wait until tomorrow, but I could not wait to start playing with this. It will take me a little bit to get used to the clicking keys, but I am finding I don't mind it, after just writing this much and, well, it does bring back memories. Also, they help me notice if I don't hit a key quite hard enough. (My high school graduation present was a used Royal portable typewriter in a horrible shade of blue--and I still dislike blue. But, I think I can get past those memories. My parents weren't cheap. They were broke at that point in time.) And now, I have probably spent more on this keyboard, even though I was amazingly fortunate to be able to get this at a discount in exchange for my honest, unbiased review, than my family spent on that typewriter.

I didn't know what to expect of this, but read what one of the other reviewers said about not needing to be a gamer to appreciate this and I can see, already, how true it is. My typing is faster and I'm just using this for the first time. And now with this and the matching mouse, I can dare to try some of the things I hear about. No, even with this pair, I won't have the fast response time of someone a few decades (well, quite a few) younger, but it will sure help even the playing field for me. I am so eager to put this through its paces and I am SO happy with it, even this soon, that I have no problem recommending anything with the Bloody name on it. And I did live in New Zealand for a while, so know that Bloody is not quite considered a polite word. Too Bloody bad. I love the keyboard and mouse!

Addendum: Two days along and I'm even happier! Wow! This thing is great!

reviews.title: Keyed Up
reviews.text: I don't do a lot of gaming but this is nicer than the old Dell keyboard I was using.  It's kind of loud but otherwise I haven't had any problems typing.  The keys light up when the computer is on and during idle times it puts on a little light show.  I use the old home row technique and haven't seen any increase in errors.  Hunt and peck types might feel differently. I already have a wrist rest so I haven't used the one that attaches to the keyboard.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard even if it's not mechanical
reviews.text:
Nice keys that feel similar to mechanical. Spacebar is set up so you can press it on the edges and it will respond just as well as hitting the middle. Larger keys such as Backspace, Return, Ctrl, and the Windows all work fine.

QWER and ASDF keys are colored orange and have a unique, bumpy texture you don't feel on the smooth black keys. I would have preferred orange keys on ASDF and JKL; since that is "Home Row" for typists. The bumpy texture would make it easier to find Home Row without looking.

Arrow keys are the common one on top and three below configuration. The keys I don't often see are a FN key and a weird key with no text label instead of a second Windows key on the right of the spacebar. The "weird" key seems to work as if you right-clicked the mouse. Finally, you will see the Print Screen (PS), Scroll Lock (SL), and Page/Break (PB) keys placed above the numeric keypad.

Aside from the extra keys, the slightly increased resistance, lit keys, and the increased weight of the keyboard itself, there isn't much else that separates this from a standard keyboard. All keys stay lit when you type and switch to rotating patterns after a minute or two of inactivity. Hit any key and you are back to fully lit.

reviews.title: Solid and positive
reviews.text:
There is a lot to like about the Bloody Gaming LED B720 Light Strike Mechanical Gaming Keyboard. Fit and feel, construction, lighting scheme, and most importantly “mechanical keys” are all first rate.

Right out of the box, you can feel the weight of this keyboard. No lightweight here. Plugging it in, there are no issues with Windows 7 to 10 – drivers are automatically detected and the keyboard is immediately available. The keys light up with a rainbow of color, with each row presenting a different color. On this B720 model, there is no way to change the color scheme, but most people will find it pleasant. In idle mode, it presents a couple of animations, upon which any key touch will immediately bring back full lighting. Lighting is dimmable to totally “off”.

Of course, you do not purchase a keyboard like this without awareness that a variety of mechanical key types exist. My other keyboard is a Tt esport MEKA Cherry Brown with Cherry MX Brown switches. Cherry MX brown switches have a moderate travel distance, with some tactile feedback for keystroke and a definite bottom and light clicking noise. For me, Brown switches provide a nice blend for gaming and touch typing. The Bloody B720 has an Infrared Optic Switch exclusively developed by Bloody, and indicated to be closest to a Cherry MX Black switch. For me, comparing the Optic Switch to Cherry Brown switches, the throw distance seems shorter for the Bloody keys, with less resistance and the bottom just slightly softer. Click wise, it is mechanically positive, but noise wise less than the brown switch. Personally, I find both very desirable for the way I type and game, and struggle with finding a preference between “Optic black” and Cherry Brown.

On other aspects of the keyboard, the Bloody B720 comes with textured and differently colored gaming silicone keys, along with a nice little left hand support for same. The quick guide provides limited documentation and the Bloody website for software download. While I did not spend a lot of time with the software, it allows macros and some customization—although no changes to lighting for the B720.

If there is anything someone might not like about the Bloody B720 it would probably be the font selection for the keys (a bit sci-fi) along with an inability to control the lighting a bit more fully. For me, it is fine, I like the way it behaves during use and in its more animated modes.

Bloody Gaming may or may not be right about their own proprietary keys becoming popular, since gamers have definite preferences and a multitude of experiences with a variety of keyboards. I can say, that for the type of work and play I do on a daily basis, I would recommend this keyboard to anyone thinking about a mechanical keyboard. The Bloody B720 is an attractive solid keyboard providing a great mechanical key typing and gaming experience.

features: ▼ Mechanical keyboard with Cherry MX Red Switches (Linear): A linear, lighter but does not have tactile bump and audible click found comparing with Cherry MX Blue switches. Cherry MX Red is ideal for gaming where the low articulation force and linear path allow for quick successive key presses. Typing with Cherry MX Red switches is similar to browns, though it does lack tactile feedback and It is always the best preference for gamers.
▼ A durable computer keyboard with PBT Double Shot keycaps for Ergonomic Keycap Shapes designed to provide you with durable, long lasting and comfortable typing experience.

▼ Plug-And-Play Simplicity. : Setup takes seconds—you simply plug the wireless receiver into a USB port and start using your keyboard right away without software. 100% Anti-ghosting with 6-key Rollover and convenient FN Multimedia Shortcuts Keys.

▼ Other Features : Original Cherry MX Switch-Each Reliable Switches Rated for 50 million Typing Times. Using 2.4Ghz Wireless can Connectivity-Up to 6 Month Long Standby Time. Product structure include the Smart Power Indicator Light and 2 Adjustable Feetstand Height.
▼ What You Can Get: iKBC Cherry MX Red switch mechanical keyboard x1, 2.4G wireless dongle x1 (equipped inside battery area), AAA batteries x2, dust cover x1, key puller x1, user manual x1.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text:
It meets all my needs. I got it to replace a wireless Velocifire keyboard whose internal battery was dying. I had to plug my old one in everyday as I couldn’t see the key labels without the backlight, and it’s built in battery life decreased from 5 to 1 days over a couple years. So it was essentially wired, while I prefer the wireless aesthetic.

This one avoids the backlight killing the battery life as well as internal battery degradation. I can see the key labels fine without a backlight, and it takes replaceable batteries. Battery life should be months without the backlight, and it even has a low battery warning light. No cord to deal with.

Seems well built, heavy, and doesn’t slide on the desk. Nice price point. Easy to set up. It even has multiple tilt angle (height) options, although I like it flat. TKL is great to minimize the distance to reach the mouse, improving ergonomics.

I went from brown to red switches as that’s all that was in stock, and it was an easy change, although I could tell a slight difference initially.

reviews.title: Quite Loud
reviews.text:
If you can't stand the sound of a clacking keyboard, this isn't for you. It can get quite loud, especially when you're typing fairly quickly. This is probably better for techies who like the intense sound and the tactile key interface. The lighting is also nice. I can recommend this keyboard for those who either live or work alone.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided helps make your decision a little easier.

reviews.title: A No-Cable Classic For Writers
reviews.text:
I am not lying: This is hands down the best keyboard for writing I have used in the last twenty years. I liked the older acrylic Apple keyboard and I own a mechanical keyboard with click-switches (blue), but this one makes touch typing so much more comfortable.
I wasn’t sure if I would like the tactile keys without click, because I actually like the click. They are just very loud. Logitech’s K845 (with Cherry MX red) is a great keyboard as well, but louder and has a cable.
I wish this keyboard would be backlit though. Black keyboards in front of big screens need some sort of illumination. I got one of these USB LED lights that I plugged behind my monitor, that half-solves the problem by providing some backlight.
Still it’s awesome to write on it. It’s focus is to be a mechanical keyboard. So much that it seems that it skipped the product design. It looks like an OEM-Keyboard from 1995. That’s in a way uncompromisingly functional, but next to a modern flat butterfly keyboard it looks like someone parked an old Volvo next to a Miata.
The quality is good. The keycaps are heavy and lettering sharp. It might be confusing for some that the ‘upper’ characters are printed next to the main set in the same size.
Battery life seems not to be a problem. I am pretty much writing all day, and I’m still on the first set of batteries that came in the package.
PROS AND CONS
+ Precise keyboard for touch-typing
+ Quiet enough for shared work areas
+ Height adjustable
+ Great quality
+ Long Battery life
+ Connects via dongle, not Bluetooth (yes, for me that’s a pro. Bluetooth sucks)
+ Compact with no number pad

- Chunky design
- No backlight
- Very heavy
- Vague keycap lettering
- No number pad (*conflict*)
I can recommend this keyboard to anyone who is typing over a long amount of times. It takes a while to get used to, but once adjusted it’s less tiring than modern butterfly or rubber cap keyboards, because the keys don’t bottom out that easily, and feel better to the fingertips. I took a few photos. I hope it helps to make a decision.

reviews.title: Silent Cherry Red Switches Work Well With Family Room Computer
reviews.text:
While the cherry MX red keys are often used for gaming, I am reviewing this as a typing keyboard in a family room, where the loud clackity clack of a keyboard is unwelcome. And I find this keyboard to be a great solution for such an environment.

This keyboard paired so effortlessly with my Mac that I wasn’t even aware it happened. I checked the keys for their mapping, and found them to be as one would expect for a Windows keyboard—just the usual issue with the non-Apple standard keys near the Shift key.

It is not “full-size,” but 80% size, so it is somewhat smaller, but not so much so that you fee cramped.

They keys are as silent as advertised. And while an old-school touch typist might miss the “click” feedback of a mechanical keyboard, these keys have decent travel, so if you are typing, you will have little doubt that you have actually engaged the key. (I can’t talk to gaming with these.)

I have had no trouble with the bluetooth connection, nor the responsiveness while typing.

If I have any reliability issues, I will be sure to revisit this review as necessary.

title: Coral Sea Keycaps 108 PBT Heat Sublimation OEM Profile Keycap for Cherry Mx Gateron Kailh Switch Mechanical Keyboard
features: 【PBT Dye Sublimation】With high-quality PBT keycaps and an anti-grease coating, these 108-key keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing. Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.
【Ukiyoe Style】Exquisite Ukiyo-e style bring a new experience. the keycaps are crafted with extra-thick walls fit for intensive gaming that produce satisfying, solid keystrokes.

【Excellent Craftsmanship】Using injection molding technology, waterproof and dustproof design, easy to clean up dirt. Floating keycap design, uniform light transmission, making the lighting effect obvious.
【OEM Profile Keycaps】 This keycap set is OEM profile which is higher than Cherry profile. It is not easy to fatigue after long-term use and has better comfort.
【Warm Tips】Keycaps only, The keyboard in the picture is only for the demo and is not for sale.

reviews.title: Looks great, types great
reviews.text:
The caps are thick and wide so typing on them is a pleasure. I also love the oriental graphics of ocean waves and whales. The only gripe would be the packaging box is a bit robust in need of better designs and material. Otherwise, this is a great product. Oh also better font selection would be better too, especially the non-capitalized letters on the pause vs page up vs page down vs delete vs insert buttons. They seem inconsistent.

I would pay an extra $5 if this keycap set supports 65% or 68% design.

reviews.title: You get what you pay for
reviews.text:
Overall it's a decent set and a good value. However, I noticed a number of stains on the keys, incorrect legends (the n in the End key), reversed prints (colon/semicolon and apostrophe/quotation were swapped), and misaligned legends (the F9 is not in line with the other F keys).

I would probably not buy from this seller or manufacturer again due to this.

reviews.title: Not great
reviews.text: My son got it for gaming and we returned it after one day because the keys were sticking. That’s clearly not ideal for a gamer.

title: EPOMAKER Mix 1.8m Coiled Type-C to USB A TPU Mechanical Keyboard Space Cable with Detachable Aviator Connector for Gaming Keyboard and Cellphone(Grey)
features: 【Epomaker MIX Cable For Keyboards and More】Ideas coming from our community, Epomaker custom MIX cable is designed to matches your keyboard, no matter what type of setup you’re working with. Choose from our different color options, it comes easy for you to match your keyboard and top off your setup. Besides that, this cable features all you want and need: Double-shielded USB 2.0 compliant, 61-gauge, 4-conductor, etc

【Durable and Flexible】Epomaker custom MIX cable is durable and flexible due to the strict selection of the durable techflex and PP double-sleeves raw materials. The coiled design enables the cables easily to integrate into a wide range of spaces

【USB-C Interface, USB 2.0/3.0 Compatible】For versatile connectivity, each one is equipped with a detachable aviator port to accommodate different connectors. Included in this run is a USB-C connector cable to fit modern boards

【Various Colors Choices】The cable is designed in various vivid colors to match different keyboard trend. Epomaker custom MIX cable is dedicated to adding more vibrant energy to your daily life and work. Solid color, contrast colors, or one with various colors like a rainbow? It is up to you! Get one to match your keyboard right away!

reviews.title: That's not what I expected
reviews.text: The cable does not have the diameter of the expected coils and the aviator connector is not of good quality. I don't think it will last long.

reviews.title: Nice looking and a great add for keyboard enthusiasts!
reviews.text:
Do you need a cable with an aviator connection? No, but do you want one? Yes! I like the mesh sleave on the cable, that gives a premium look and a perceived ruggedness. I chose the grey so whatever keyboard I hook up (or any USB-c peripheral) it looks nice.

Only ding I would give it is that if the keyboard needs to be to the left the cable off the coils is on top and does not look as nice. If the cable is oriented to the right, it looks better. I don't have any major issues here, but it is something that shoudl be in the product description maybe.

Overall, really nice and glad I bought it!

title: MELETRIX WS Grey Tactile Mechanical Keyboard Lubed Switches(35pcs)
description: WS Grey Tactile Mechanical Keyboard Lubed Switches
features: Nylon Top and bottom housing, POM silent tactile stem
Double stage spring: 63.5g
Total travel: 3.50±0.3mm Pre-travel: 2.00±0.5mm Bottom-out force: 63.5
Factory lubed

reviews.title: Can be used stock!
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:528307786b36a4c2c9833ddd389101cb]] They come with a small amount of lube pre applied. Usually, prelubed switches are inconsistent in my experience but these ones are really good. I decided to not lube them at all. EDIT: got a few more batches, they're not as consistent. But seller was nice enough to get them replaced.

The stems are long pole. As a result, it has a nice bottom out sound.

It was noticeably heavier coming from a 50g switch to these that are 63g, but overtime I got adjusted and had no problems typing with them.

Overall a great buy, though I wish it was priced more competitively.

Using on Drop Preonic with MT3 Camillo, plateless, tape mod

reviews.title: Nice linears
reviews.text:
Starting off not sure who's at fault for this as it's only fulfilled by Amazon but one of my bags was missing a few switches (one bag was open and all the switches were rattling around inside the amazon pouch it arrived in) I counted 5 missing and there wasn't a silica gel packet either, the other bags were fine.
complaint aside they feel really nice and the factory lube seems to be well done as wish they had a "3 pin" option and different switch amounts 35 2 is too few and 3 is too many (unless some get lost in transit like mine lol) 90 would be a good option for the TKL/75% gang

reviews.title: Will be Using Right Away
reviews.text: In building & repairing computers these key switches are used very often. Mainly for high-end (over $100) keyboards that the serious gamers use. What they do is tough on the keyboards and often switches break or become less responsive which may lead to a gamer losing a match. These snap right in to the sockets and can instantly rebuild the whole keyboard like new. I realize they would not be used by your everyday person or casual gamer but when it gets serious they are  the switches to be used.

reviews.title: Nice Linear
reviews.text: These are nice and smooth and sound wonderful.  I'm not a linear fan usually, but I do like them in my modifier keys (shift, backspace, enter, and space) because I don't like the return sound a tactile makes while in a larger key.  These are perfect for that as they don't have a high pitch and there's no spring ping to even think of!  I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a lighter-weight linear switch with some style.

reviews.title: Great Stock!
reviews.text: The switches sounds great and feels good to use. The lube in this is not bad at all and you don't hear any ping or scratchiness. The bottom out is much more than I am used to, but I do enjoy it otherwise.

title: 141 Keys Botanical Keycaps Cherry Profile PBT Dye Sub Keycap Set Suitable for 61/64/68/84/87/96/104/108 Cherry Mx Switches Mechanical Gaming Keyboard
features: 【PBT Keycaps】These 141 cherry profile keycaps are made of thick PBT material, with a matte texture and anti-grease coating, which can provide good durability and typing.

【Cherry Profile】This set of pbt keycaps uses the cherry height, which is higher than the XDA profile. It's ergonomically designed, so you will not feel tired even if you type or play games for a long time.
【Rugged and Durable】The thickness of these keycaps are about 1.4~1.5mm, so the keycaps are very durable. The thick PBT material will effectively extend the service life of the keycaps.

【Wide Selection】These keycaps are suitable for most keyboards, such as 61/64/68/84/87/96/104/108 cherry mx switches mechanical Keyboards.If you are not sure whether this keycaps set is fit for your keyboard, please consult customer service.
【Warm Tips】This product is a set of keycaps, not a keyboard. If the keycaps are missing or cracked, don't worry, we'll send you new replacement keys.

reviews.title: OMG great keycap set!
reviews.text:
I have been looking for these sets for the longest time, outside of GB and these guys have made a solid replica. i am so impressed with the quality and feel of these Botanical keycap sets that the premium sets can remain on the shelf. They look so nice and very very close and clean to the originals.

the only way you can tell that they are not GMK is by looking at the bottom. The dye-sub is a little weak in the finish but you never look under the hood.

If you like the greenery landscape or the color great fanatic this is the best set. It's super clean, subtle, and sounds great! I have this set on my KBD67 Lite and it sounds so much better than my ABS keycaps i paid $80 for. The texture is also very nice and I seem to be keen on this rough like texture than the ABS smoother feel. It kind of is matte if that makes sense. The legends on all the alphas are uniform, no thickness issues. The ,.? and other modifiers might have a thinner font but negligible.

I can't say enough what a great first impression these sets have given me and if they produce an "Oblivian" replica i'm jumping on it as well. Pictures to come, after I pickup my desk from all the loose keycaps.

reviews.title: Great sound
reviews.text: Don’t wait years for a gmk set these are just as good lol

title: Kraken Coiled Keyboard Cable - Artisan Coiled USB C Cable with Aviator Connector for Mechanical Gaming Keyboards (Pure White)
features: Premium Artisan Designs in colors to fit every gaming setup!

Double Sleeved for enhanced looks and durability!

reviews.title: Excellent quality cable and connectors, but color is more purple than blue
reviews.text:
Update: Oddly enough, this did not work with a Drop Alt keyboard. It worked with a few other keyboards just fine though. Just a word of warning for anyone pairing this cable with a Drop Alt (or maybe any other Drop keyboard).

This is the third coiled cable I have ordered, and it's definitely the best quality overall. The cable itself is thick and good quality. The coil is a bit firm, but nothing too unusual. The intermediate connector is all metal and heavy. Both sides of the intermediate connector can be unthreaded: The side going to the USB-C connector, and the side going to the USB connector.

One interesting thing I noticed was that the end connectors have a color matched almost shrink wrap thickness strain protection around them. The USB side is the most noticeable, and the USB-C side is a bit thicker. As long as there is enough strain protection, I actually prefer the less bulky end connections.

The only nit is the color. Even though it looked dark purplish in the product listing, I was hoping that it would be more blue in real life. It's not, it's an attractive dark purple. I would assume the color represented in the product listing is accurate, in case you're wondering if there is any color variation from the listing photos to real life.

I do like the look and feel of the keyboard and mouse, the disconnection issues are my only problem. (and since another wireless keyboard and mouse don't have the problem on the same PC, I know I don't have interference issues or badly set settings)

reviews.title: Really looks nice!
reviews.text: Looks really nice, not really that great at allowing RGB to shine through but overall I think it's a nice, beautiful addition to any keyboard.  Mine fit perfectly and adds a certain nice uniqueness to a keyboard that I, as an artist, really enjoy.  I'd definitely recommend, this is really nice looking.

reviews.title: Really Nice Artisan, But May Fit Tightly
reviews.text: The quality of this artisan is really good for the price, and it looks quite nice when illuminated by backlighting as well. The keycap does fit quite tightly but I did manage to get it to fit though some may have issues with fitment. Overall it's a nice artisan but may fit tightly so be aware of that when ordering.

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

It also has split spacebar keys that you might need. A keycap set that makes your DIY journey worry-free

reviews.title: Kawaii !!
reviews.text: Comes with all the keys caps you need, love how smooth it feels. You may need to dim your keyboard brightness, found the light through the clear key caps can hurt your eyes after a while.

reviews.title: Exceptionally perfect feel EXCEPT no way to find your position!
reviews.text:
I wanted to love these key caps. They look great! They feel superb and their smooth texture is exactly what I wanted.

However, two major flaws: I didn't even know this was a thing to consider, but they don't have bumps on the positioning keys "F" and "J". So if you type a lot and remove your hands from the keys, you need to look down every time to find your position. This interrupts my workflow as a transcriptionist quite a bit. The other disadvantage is that the smooth feel of the material also makes it impossible to keep skin oil, snack residues, etc. from being left behind on these caps, making them somewhat difficult to grip after the first half hour.

reviews.title: Really nice visual upgrade
reviews.text:
I wanted to upgrade my keyboard’s appearance, and after much Googling I settled on this product. I have a full size 104 key keyboard, and looking at keycaps on various websites makes it seem kinda complicated to know which set I’d need for my specific keyboard (Das Keyboard 4). Some Ctrl keys are wider than others, Spacebars shorter than others, etc. and what I liked about this set is that it includes different length keys and you just use the one that fits your keyboard and ignore the rest.

This is my first set of keycaps so I can’t really speak with any knowledge to the quality of the keys, but I’m really happy with how they look. They feel similar to the ones that came with my keyboard but look muuuuch better.

reviews.title: Fantastic keycap set
reviews.text:
They finally arrived! I plan on putting these keycaps on a 75% keyboard that won't arrive for another couple of months so in the pictures, I only put it on part of my keyboard to test them out. I want to "preserve" that new keycap feeling later. :)

These keycaps are fairly thick and have the standard PBT texture. The doubleshot legends are quite clear and none of the spacebars are warped! This is a new profile I've never typed on and I can't find much information on the web about it so I included a lot of pictures. I compared them in the pictures to my Ducky keyboard with doubleshot ABS. They actually aren't too different from OEM keycap profile (from the Ducky) height but some of the rows are higher. They're much more shapely and the F and J keys have a deeper dish. Compared to DSA keycaps, they don't seem to be quite as deep dishey overall which I actually prefer.
The keycaps fit snug on cherry mx brown stems, more so than my Ducky keycaps. They aren't hard to put on though. Some of the stems have a small part of plastic missing from the MX cross area but it's only a handful of the keycaps and it didn't effect the fit. I've included photos of this as well.
Overall they're great keycaps that I won't be using yet until my keyboard for them arrives.

They are gorgeous, sophisticated, and they FEEL better than any other pudding keycaps I've ever had. No wobble, no cheap plastic sound. They add to the sound of the board and typing experience. Superior to HyperX. I have them paired with my Kailh Box Whites and I couldn't be happier.

title: Litorange Silicone 500PCS 40A Clear Universal O-Ring Switch Dampeners Keycap Sealing Gasket Washer (Better Than Rubber) for Cherry MX Switch Keyboard and Mechanical Keyboard Keys Dampers
description:
★Products include：
500PCS 8mm * 5mm * 1.5mm O-Ring 2PCS Keyboard Brushes 2PCS Keycap Puller   ★ Litorange Keyboard Kit is the essential accessory kit to improve key actuation speed, reduce bottoming out sound, reduce the shock on your fingers, and to maintain a clean keyboard. ★ 500 Silicone O-Ring Dampeners limit noise on keys and provide faster actuation with a 2mm thickness | 40A Shore (Soft) Transparent O-Rings allow LED Backlighting to shine brightly through keys. ★ Round Keycap Puller lets you remove mechanical keys with little effort and no restraint | Cleaning brush and cloth help maintain the cleanliness and pristine condition of your keyboard. ★ Plastic Box holds all mod kit components and your gaming mouse for easy and safe storage. ★ 40A (SOFT) O-RINGS: Softer O-rings provide a softer padded landing with every keystroke that reduces the impact on your fingers and eliminates the bottoming out "clack" sound of your keyboard.
★ Sound Dampeners & Maintenance Kit
Every mechanical keyboard needs a little tender love and care.  The Litorange Mod Kit includes every tool necessary to clean, improve, and silence your mechanical gaming keyboard.  Reduce the loud clacking sound for quieter typing late at night and maintain the look of your keyboard. The Mechanical Keyboard Mod Kit comes with 500 O-Ring silicone sound dampeners.  Transparent rings allow your keyboard's LED lighting to shine through without obstruction.  The 2mm thick O-Rings provide faster actuation time to help you beat the competition.
features: ★ Material: Silicone (Better than rubber). Quantity: 500 Pieces. Size: 8 mm/0.3 inch outer diameter, 5 mm/0.2 inch in inner diameter and 1.5 mm/ 0.06 inch in thickness.
★ Litorange Keyboard Kit is the essential accessory kit to improve key actuation speed, reduce bottoming out sound, reduce the shock on your fingers, and to maintain a clean keyboard.
★ Can be used across a wide temperature range from -65 degrees F (-54 degrees C) to 450 degrees F (232 degrees C).
★ 500 Silicone O-Ring Dampeners limit noise on keys and provide faster actuation with a 2mm thickness | 40A Shore (Soft) Transparent O-Rings allow LED Backlighting to shine brightly through keys.
★ 40A (SOFT) O-Ring: Softer O-rings provide a softer padded landing with every keystroke that reduces the impact on your fingers and eliminates the bottoming out "clack" sound of your keyboard.

reviews.title: Not for Certain Noise
reviews.text:
Unfortunately these o-rings didn't help with the noise from my mechanical keyboard and switches. The noise from my keyboard comes from the sound the springs make when the button pops back up, and these o-rings work to muffle sound when the keys are pressed down. For keyboards with raised keys that don't bottom out, like mine, and for switches that make more noise when they come back up instead of when they go down, the o-rings are not effective for reducing noise.

Otherwise, the product is good, and I'm keeping the o-rings for when I eventually change out the switches on my keyboard entirely.

reviews.title: awesome!
reviews.text: awesome set of o-rings for the price.

reviews.title: Really nice modification for my keyboard
reviews.text: These cut down the travel and damp the clicks in my moonlander, great value.

reviews.title: Good value rings kit
reviews.text:
I was setting up two keyboards with lighter switches, and think that o-rings are important when you drop the force. This kit was a good value, and I like the brushes to clean the keyboards.

I would recommend a decent set of tweezers to push the rings down onto the key posts. I had a plastic set that was perfect for it ( they came with a pair of Moondrop IEMs ).

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Very good O-rings

reviews.title: Solid
reviews.text: This keyboard and mouse set is solid.  They have a durable aluminum body.  The keys are mechanical in look, but press easy without any noise.  The colors are nice and shine through the keys so you can see which letter it is in the dark.  It gives off just the right amount of light.  They look slick together on a desk and operate well.  Great little set.

reviews.title: Solid
reviews.text: This keyboard and mouse set is solid.  They have a durable aluminum body.  The keys are mechanical in look, but press easy without any noise.  The colors are nice and shine through the keys so you can see which letter it is in the dark.  It gives off just the right amount of light.  They look slick together on a desk and operate well.  Great little set.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard!!
reviews.text:
Updated review.. The mouse quit working after about 12 hours of use. This is disappointing because when it worked it was nice and responsive and was comfortable. When I left the original review everything worked fine..

This keyboard looks and works good. The back lighting makes it easy to see in the dark and the keys are precise and feel good while typing. The real aluminum frame is sturdy and looks good too. The phone holder is a nice touch. I would say it's worth it for the price.

title: dagaladoo XVX 189 Keys Double Shot Keycaps, PBT Custom Keyboard Keycaps Full Set, XVX Profile Keycaps for 60% 65% 70% 100% Cherry Gateron MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard, Black/Purple
features: Full 189 keys PBT keycaps set, including 108 standard keys, 61 supplementary keys and 20 invert color supplementary keys for most 61/64/66/68/84/87/96/98/100/104/108 mechanical keyboards.
Double shot design keycaps, made from two colors material molded into each others, make the keycaps characters maintain the vibrance and saturation, clear and not fade.
XVX profile keycaps, ergonomic height design similar than Cherry profile, while increasing the contact surface between the finger and keycaps, bring you a comfortable typing experience.
Made from high content PBT material, 0.2cm extra-thick keycaps wall, which is provide top-notch durability, excellent touch and grease resistance.
Minimalist theme style and classic color matching, with a large number of personalized complement keys, you can DIY different styles of keyboards according to your own ideas.

reviews.title: Fits Royal Kludge 90% and Keychron K10!
reviews.text: I like all the extras. The quality of the PBT is grainy in texture, so if that's the kind of thing you're in to, then these won't disappoint. Not shine through, although the advertisement never claims they are. No light is coming through these from your board. The XVX profile is nice. Many extra key options- this set has fit several boards from several brands- not something you see for the price point! Quality printing and nice font on the caps. The keys feel good to type on when used with a clicky or linear switch. They are a bit heavy, so a lighter switch wouldn't be a bad option. I don't like to use these on tactile switches due to the mushy that these caps add to the keys. O Rings did not help these keys IMO.

reviews.title: Random assortment of loose keys
reviews.text: Expected a full set of keycaps, instead received a box containing three bags of loose keys, missing multiple letters, numbers, function keys, etc. Might be good for a concept art project, but not for a working keyboard.

reviews.title: Some keys misaligned with switch stems.
reviews.text: The keys have a nice touch, sound and printing however some keys, particularly my "O" key are a misaligned with the switch stem causing it to stick up a little higher than the "I" and "P" on either side. I did not mistake a "0" for an "O".

reviews.title: Seem to be good quality, but I hate the XVX profile
reviews.text:
These seems to be good quality keycaps, I have no issues with them at all. I have put them on multiple boards now, and I haven't had any break or scratch. Colors are accurate and legends are clear. No complaints there.

My one gripe is the XVX profile. It takes a lot of getting used to (like any new profile would), but the surface area of the keys is much larger than most other profiles. Therefore, when typing, it feels as if your fingers have to travel significantly further to reach the next key. It also makes it much, much easier to fat finger extra keys. If I had realized how much more surface area there was, I would not have purchased these. However, they do look great, they were cheap, and they sound pretty good too.

reviews.title: Great keycaps, andd even better customer service!
reviews.text: I wanted to find some PBT keycaps that were double-shot and gave some aesthetic flair to my $15 108 full sized mech keyboard with brown switches.  They fit just fine, and give a different, yet, pleasing sounds and feel to the keyboard.  I wanted non-see through keycaps in order to hide most of the LED rainbow glare.  The backlit effect is, again, very pleasing.  The keycap set that I originally ordered was missing the F9 key.  I contacted their customer service through Amazon, and got a reply back right away and arranged for a replacement set to be shipped out.  Got it the new set the day after, and it was complete.  Will purchase from this seller again whenever I need/want new keycaps.  Very confident if something is wrong, will be taken care of in a timely matter.
